citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There is a bottle of 1% ivermectin which expired on 02/20/2025 according to the label printed by the veterinarian; it is
stored in a cabinet inside the commissary building, among in-use, non-expired items. When this was brought to the
attention of facility representatives, the medication was marked as "Expired" and removed from the cabinet for disposal.
Storing expired items among non-expired items could result in administration of expired substances to animals. The use
of expired medications or medications with an unknown expiration date is not considered to be a safe or appropriate
method to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries because expired drugs may be ineffective or
produce other undesired effects. Animals may experience discomfort or distress due to unrelieved symptoms or
unexpected side effects from the use of expired medication. The program for providing adequate veterinary care shall
include the use of appropriate methods to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries. This includes
following any established procedures to ensure medication labels have expiration dates and that expired medications are
removed and cannot be administered to animals.
CORRECTED AT THE TIME OF THIS INSPECTION ON 02/19/2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.40(b)(2) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
There is a bottle of 1% ivermectin which expired on 02/20/2025 according to the label printed by the veterinarian; it is

method to prevent, control, diagnose, and treat diseases and injuries because expired drugs may be ineffective or

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
